title: Proclamation Recognizing Peace Officers Memorial Day and Police Week 2026
summary: Presidential proclamation designating observance of Peace Officers Memorial Day and Police Week in 2026. The proclamation honors law enforcement personnel who have died in the line of duty and acknowledges the contributions of active law enforcement officers to public safety and community protection.

title: Presidential Nominations Submitted to Senate for Executive Department Positions
summary: A set of nominations was transmitted to the U.S. Senate for various executive branch positions, including Deputy Secretary of the Treasury, Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency within the Department of Homeland Security, and Assistant Secretary of State for European and Eurasian Affairs, among other positions. The nominees represent various states and would require Senate confirmation to assume their respective roles in accordance with the Appointments Clause of the U.S. Constitution.

title: Proclamation Designating Military Spouse Day, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation issued to recognize and honor military spouses in 2026. The proclamation acknowledges the contributions of military families and spouses in supporting the Armed Forces throughout American history, framing their role within the context of national defense and the continuation of American institutions.

title: Proclamation Recognizing Victory Day for World War II
summary: A presidential proclamation commemorating May 8, 1945, and the conclusion of World War II in Europe. The proclamation acknowledges the military contributions of the United States Armed Forces and Allied forces in the European theater of World War II and designates Victory Day as an occasion for national recognition and remembrance of this historical event.

title: Proclamation Designating May 2026 as National Physical Fitness and Sports Month
summary: Presidential proclamation designating May 2026 as National Physical Fitness and Sports Month. The proclamation acknowledges the role of sports and fitness activities in American culture and emphasizes the importance of promoting healthy lifestyles and expanding access to physical fitness opportunities and athletic programs throughout the nation.

title: Proclamation Designating May 2026 as Jewish American Heritage Month
summary: A presidential proclamation that designates May 2026 as Jewish American Heritage Month. The proclamation acknowledges the historical contributions of Jewish Americans throughout United States history and recognizes their involvement in American civic life and values. The proclamation is issued as an annual observance to commemorate the cultural and historical significance of the Jewish American community within the broader American narrative.

title: Presidential Permit for Bridger Pipeline Expansion LLC to Construct and Operate Pipeline Facilities at U.S.-Canada Border in Montana
summary: This presidential permit authorizes Bridger Pipeline Expansion LLC, a privately held limited liability company, to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities at the international boundary between the United States and Canada in Phillips County, Montana. The permit is granted subject to specified conditions and is issued under the authority vested in the President of the United States.

title: Proclamation Designating National Fallen Firefighters Memorial Weekend 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation that designates a specific weekend as National Fallen Firefighters Memorial Weekend in 2026. The proclamation acknowledges and honors volunteer and career firefighters who have died in the line of duty, with particular recognition for those lost during the preceding year. The proclamation serves to establish an official observance for the nation to pay tribute to firefighters who have made the ultimate sacrifice while performing their duties.
